Teton-Jackson 115 kV transmission line : environmental impact statement.

The Teton-Jackson 115 kV transmission line project involves constructing a 15.8-mile power line from Teton to Jackson in Teton County, Wyoming. This project aims to upgrade the existing power infrastructure to meet increasing electricity demands in the Jackson Hole area. The line will cross scenic and environmentally sensitive areas, including the Grand Teton National Park and the National Elk Refuge. While it promises to improve power reliability and support local development, concerns have been raised about its visual impact on the landscape and potential effects on local wildlife and waterways.
Lower Valley Power and Light, Inc.
Concerned Citizens of Jackson Hole, Wyoming

The Teton-Jackson 115 kV transmission line project was proposed to address the growing electricity demands in the Jackson Hole area, which has seen increased development and tourism. However, the project faced public pushback due to concerns about its impact on the scenic landscape and local wildlife, particularly as it would cross sensitive areas like Grand Teton National Park and the National Elk Refuge. Ultimately, the project proceeded with considerations for mitigating its environmental impact.
"The most positive aspect of life in Teton County was the scenic beauty/environment of the area." — University of Wyoming Survey, 1974
"The proposed line will be visible to recreationists who may be boating, camping, sightseeing or fishing in the area." — Environmental Impact Statement, Section 2.1
